jokes.text: I-I'm sorry. I didn't mean that as a come-on. No, that's not true. I did mean that as a come-on.
jokes.setup: Mike tries to backtrack from what sounds like a come-on
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he's lying and it was totally a come-on
jokes.explanation: Nice quick reversal that shows character's honesty and awkwardness. Good economy of language.

jokes.text: I'd feel like a call girl. You didn't do anything. I'd feel like a bad call girl.
jokes.setup: Woman says she'd feel like a call girl taking money
jokes.punchline: Escalates to feeling like a BAD call girl since she didn't do anything
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation that subverts expectation. The logic is absurd but follows perfectly.

jokes.text: What would you have given me if I had stayed the night? A very sweet memory. You're better off with the hundred.
jokes.setup: Woman asks what she would have gotten for staying the night
jokes.punchline: Mike's romantic answer is immediately undercut by his honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and devastating punchline. Great character moment showing self-awareness.

jokes.text: Oh.
jokes.setup: Louie is introduced to Zena and she wanted to meet him
jokes.punchline: Long awkward beat as Louie processes this attractive woman wants to meet him
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie reaction beat. Economy is perfect - the silence says everything.

jokes.text: She ripped the shirt off my back.
jokes.setup: Louie claims he and Zena had 'another one of those nights'
jokes.punchline: The cliche phrase reveals he's lying/exaggerating their physical relationship
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's bravado masking insecurity.

jokes.text: I got nothing.
jokes.setup: Alex shows no interest in Louie's supposed sexual exploits
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately deflates and admits he has no stories
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Louie's bravado. Great economy and character work.

jokes.text: I don't do that. Yes, you do. You do it almost continuously. I don't. Yes, you do.
jokes.setup: Alex accuses Louie of doing something when he mentions Zena
jokes.punchline: Immediate back-and-forth denial that proves Alex's point
jokes.explanation: Standard but effective bit showing Louie's unconscious behavior.

jokes.text: You mean he hasn't touched you? I can't even get him to hold my hand.
jokes.setup: Alex is shocked that Louie hasn't touched Zena
jokes.punchline: It's even worse - he won't even hold her hand
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation about Louie's issues with nice women.

jokes.text: Zena's not your first girl, she's your first nice girl.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Louie's romantic problems
jokes.punchline: Alex identifies the real issue - Louie can't handle a good woman
jokes.explanation: Insightful character observation that cuts right to Louie's core issue.

jokes.text: Break up with this lovely, warm person, who obviously feels a great deal for you, and spend the rest of your life floating through meaningless affairs with cheap strangers, who you'll have to pay to satisfy your disgusting, physical lust.
jokes.setup: Louie asks for advice about his relationship problem
jokes.punchline: Alex gives brutally sarcastic 'advice' that describes exactly what Louie will do
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sarcasm that perfectly captures both characters. Long but every word lands.

jokes.text: Great advice.
jokes.setup: Alex gives devastating sarcastic advice
jokes.punchline: Louie's completely straight delivery suggests he might actually take it
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that shows Louie completely missing the sarcasm.

jokes.text: Donna Summer. We had a little spat before I met you. I couldn't take the pressure of the racial thing. And besides, she's always shaking it in front of other guys. It got to me. I told her, 'I don't want you moaning on records anymore.'
jokes.setup: Zena asks who the other woman is
jokes.punchline: Louie claims he was dating disco star Donna Summer with increasingly ridiculous details
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd lie that escalates beautifully. The details make it funnier.

jokes.text: It's just that you're too good, period.
jokes.setup: Zena suggests Louie thinks she's too good for him
jokes.punchline: Louie's blunt admission that she's just too good in general
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's rare moment of honesty about his limitations.

jokes.text: relating to you in a nonverbal manner.
jokes.setup: Louie explains he can't think of being physical with Zena
jokes.punchline: His overly clinical euphemism for sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie-speak euphemism that shows his discomfort with intimacy.

jokes.text: To tell you the truth, I've been asking myself that question. My friends have been asking me that question, my parents, the doorman's been asking me that question.
jokes.setup: Louie asks why she's nuts about him
jokes.punchline: Everyone in her life is asking the same question
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that builds to the doorman detail. Self-deprecating humor.

jokes.text: You touch me.
jokes.setup: Long buildup about why Zena loves Louie despite everyone questioning it
jokes.punchline: The ironic answer since he literally won't touch her
jokes.explanation: Beautifully ironic given the entire conversation about him not touching her. Perfect emotional/comedic beat.

jokes.text: Holy crap.
jokes.setup: Zena's emotional declaration that he 'touches' her
jokes.punchline: Louie's stunned, crude reaction to genuine sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie reaction to genuine emotion - crude but shows he's moved.

jokes.text: Take me to the place you cry.
jokes.setup: Zena tells him she goes home and cries after their dates
jokes.punchline: Louie's romantic but awkward way of asking to come up
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly awkward romantic line that's both sweet and crude simultaneously.

jokes.text: I can't go in there, Zena! Let me out! Let me out!
jokes.setup: Louie finally gets invited up to Zena's apartment
jokes.punchline: He immediately panics and wants to escape the elevator
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Louie's panic when faced with intimacy.

jokes.text: 'It would mean a lot to me.'
jokes.setup: Zena asks for a goodbye kiss
jokes.punchline: Louie mockingly repeats her request, showing his discomfort with emotion
jokes.explanation: Typical Louie defense mechanism - mocking sincere emotion to avoid dealing with it.

jokes.text: You can go now, Louie. That's easy for you to say.
jokes.setup: After their kiss, Zena casually dismisses Louie
jokes.punchline: Louie's stunned reaction suggests the kiss affected him more than expected
jokes.explanation: Good reversal - now Louie doesn't want to leave after being dismissive all episode.

jokes.text: You'll drink a glass of water very quickly. Somebody is going to mistake you for a girl. You'll do the cancan in a green shirt, wearing a catcher's mask
jokes.setup: Jim describes his prophetic dream about Alex's death
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous and specific predictions
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd prophecy that sets up the rest of the bit. Specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That's the silliest thing I ever heard. Aha!
jokes.setup: Alex dismisses the prophecy while drinking water quickly
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately points out Alex just fulfilled the first prediction
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with Alex unknowingly fulfilling the prophecy while denying it.

jokes.text: No, this is not Mary. Do I sound like a Mary? You have the wrong number.
jokes.setup: Phone rings and Alex answers during prophecy discussion
jokes.punchline: Caller mistakes Alex for a woman named Mary - second prediction fulfilled
jokes.explanation: Perfect coincidental fulfillment of the second prediction. Well-executed escalation.

jokes.text: Louie, if I'm going to die, I'm going to die as I lived, dancing the cancan in a green shirt and a catcher's mask.
jokes.setup: Alex decides to embrace fate rather than run from it
jokes.punchline: The absurd implication that he normally dances cancan in this outfit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist line suggesting this ridiculous activity is how Alex normally lives. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Louie... I didn't die.
jokes.setup: 7:00 passes and Alex is still alive after doing all the predicted actions
jokes.punchline: Alex's matter-of-fact observation of his continued existence
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of the obvious, deflating all the built-up tension.

jokes.text: Ignatowski's a boob.
jokes.setup: The prophecy turns out to be wrong
jokes.punchline: Louie's blunt assessment of Jim's prophetic abilities
jokes.explanation: Typical Louie bluntness. Perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Nice cancan.
jokes.setup: Alex thanks Louie for the compliment
jokes.punchline: Casual acknowledgment of the absurd thing he just did
jokes.explanation: Nice casual callback that treats the absurd as normal.

jokes.text: Reiger, did you see it? It was hideous!
jokes.setup: Something scary appears at the door during the 'death' moment
jokes.punchline: Louie's terrified reaction to whatever was there
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment with Louie's characteristic cowardice.

jokes.text: But if I were to tell you my fantasy, it would knock you out.
jokes.setup: Elaine says she won't share her fantasy
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts herself by hyping how amazing it would be
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Elaine's need for attention despite claiming otherwise.

jokes.text: We always play cards. We play cards every day. That's all we do is play cards. Play cards.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests playing another hand of poker
jokes.punchline: Frustrated repetition emphasizing their boring routine
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about repetitive routine but not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: Elaine, we're trying to play cards here. Come on! Buzz off.
jokes.setup: Elaine performs an elaborate musical number
jokes.punchline: The guys' complete dismissal of her performance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing the disconnect between Elaine's fantasy and reality.

jokes.text: Good night, Mr. Walters!
jokes.setup: End of episode with typical dispatcher duties
jokes.punchline: Louie being polite to someone offscreen, showing his different face with authority
jokes.explanation: Nice character detail showing Louie's deference to his boss versus his treatment of the drivers.

jokes.text: Good night, Mr. Walters!
jokes.setup: End of episode, Louie addressing someone off-screen
jokes.punchline: Presumably addressing the audience/show creator with characteristic Louie attitude
jokes.explanation: Meta ending moment with Louie breaking fourth wall in character-appropriate way
